patterns.name: Energy Sector Regulatory Lag
patterns.description: Energy sector clients consistently operating 18–24 months behind regulatory compliance curves. NERC CIP amendments are known ahead of enforcement but not acted upon until audit pressure.
patterns.implication: Early engagement 12+ months before enforcement deadline creates strong ROI case for clients. Position Sustainable Gaps as the proactive partner, not reactive responder.
patterns.recommendedAction: Build a regulatory calendar product — alert clients to upcoming deadlines with 12-month lead time.

patterns.name: IT/OT Convergence Blind Spot
patterns.description: Across energy and manufacturing clients, IT and OT teams operate in silos. Physical access controls and cyber controls are managed separately with no unified risk model.
patterns.implication: Neither the IT team nor the OT team sees the full exposure picture. Sustainable Gaps occupies a unique position as the integrator of both views.
patterns.recommendedAction: Develop a combined IT/OT/Physical Security Assessment as a standard service offering. Price as premium engagement.

patterns.name: Small-Team Operational Overload
patterns.description: Multiple team members (Ava 88%, Daniel 82%) showing workload above sustainable threshold. As client count grows without capacity expansion, delivery quality risk increases.
patterns.implication: Need either: (a) agent capacity expansion before next engagement intake, or (b) structured prioritization protocol to triage lower-priority work.
patterns.recommendedAction: Leo to model next capacity ceiling. Ava to implement intake throttle if ceiling < 2 new engagements.

patterns.name: Datacenter Build Wave — Physical Security Gap
patterns.description: AI workload growth is driving unprecedented datacenter construction globally. Physical security assessments are being skipped or deferred to post-build phase — creating exploitable gaps in facilities housing critical AI infrastructure.
patterns.implication: This is a 5-10 year macro trend. The firm that establishes methodology and reputation for datacenter physical security assessment now will dominate as the sector matures.
patterns.recommendedAction: Begin building datacenter-specific assessment methodology. Target first datacenter client by Q3 2026.
